signals.name: Lateral acceleration
signals.description: Working Ford lateral-acceleration parameter. Formula raw/50; observed live values around -0.14 to 0.4.

signals.name: Short term fuel trim bank 1 test
signals.description: Generic SAE short-term fuel trim Bank 1 candidate exposed for Everest diesel comparison testing. Diesel engines may not use petrol-style closed-loop fuel trim, so this may be unsupported or unhelpful.

signals.name: Short term fuel trim bank 2 test
signals.description: Generic SAE short-term fuel trim Bank 2 candidate exposed for Everest diesel comparison testing. The 2.0L diesel may not report this like a petrol engine; keep as test-only unless it proves useful.

signals.name: Generic MAP
signals.description: Generic SAE MAP value. Confirmed responding; latest test showed 15.

signals.name: DPF / exhaust filter fullness

signals.name: Distance since DPF regen
signals.description: Everest-confirmed. Formula raw / 10 km. Counted up with driving distance, reset to 0 km at regen completion, then immediately started counting up again. Promoted from validation after live Pelican testing.

signals.name: Transmission fluid temperature Everest div16
signals.description: Promoted to primary Everest transmission-fluid-temperature candidate after Pelican drive comparison. Same 7E1 221E1C TCM response as the 5/72 value; div16 warm-up and operating range looked more plausible against coolant, oil temperature, speed/load, and normal 10-speed behaviour. Validate against FORScan/factory scan data when available.

signals.name: Transmission fluid temperature 5/72 compare
signals.description: Comparison/legacy scaling for the same 7E1 221E1C TCM response. Kept for validation only; current Pelican drive comparison suggests this reads lower than expected, so div16 is now the preferred active Everest transmission-temperature candidate.

signals.name: Transmission gear engaged
